_README: Tag library for player profile badges. Manually assigned per prospect. 37 tags across 3 layers (skills, outlook, concerns). No auto-fire logic — pure scout authoring. Icon values are semantic names mapped to lucide-react in the UI; missing icons fall through to a generic dot.
_layerOrder: Skills first (the most-assigned/most-visible band), then Outlook (the high-stakes gold layer), then Concerns (red flags) so the visual rhythm goes neutral → bullish → bearish.

outlook.label: Outlook
outlook.usageRule: Restrictive. Single player should rarely wear more than 2-3 gold tags. The gold layer earns its weight by staying scarce.

_conflictsRule: Pairs that should not co-fire. Conflicts are bidirectional but listed once for readability — the editor enforces both directions. Tag conflicts are also stored on the individual tag's `conflicts` field so a per-tag lookup is cheap.

tags.name: Lather Scorer
tags.description: Heat-up scorer — rhythm builds through the game. Volume + reps unlock the bag; rare for them to score in spurts.

tags.name: Volume Scorer
tags.description: Gets points via sheer attempts. Will take 20+ shots a night because the role demands it — efficiency may or may not follow.

tags.name: Tuxedo Scorer
tags.description: Effortless scoring — pristine craft, clean release, minimal effort. Makes hard buckets look routine.

tags.name: Midrange Mastery
tags.description: Elite in the in-between game — pull-ups, runners, mid-range fadeaways. The lost-art tag.

tags.name: Pull-up Threat
tags.description: Dangerous off the dribble. Self-creation gravity that bends defenses on every screen action.

tags.name: Downhill Dynamo
tags.description: Gets to the rim with force. First-step burst and finishing through contact, not around it.

tags.name: Crafty Finisher
tags.description: Wraparounds, reverse layups, body english at the rim. Wins finishes via angles and touch, not athleticism.

tags.name: Float Game
tags.description: Floater / runner is a real weapon. Counter to drop coverage; converts the in-between space.

tags.name: Post Moves
tags.description: Has actual back-to-basket repertoire — drop step, hook, up-and-under. Not just a face-up game.

tags.name: Foul Magnet
tags.description: Draws fouls at an elite rate. FTr is a real skill here — manipulates angles, sells contact, lives at the line.

tags.name: Catch-and-shoot Threat
tags.description: Reliable spot-up shooter. Defenders have to close out hard — generates value without needing touches.

tags.name: Movement Shooter
tags.description: Threats off screens, hand-offs, relocations. Shooting that scales with the offense around them.

tags.name: Vertical Spacer
tags.description: Rim-running / lob threat. Spaces the floor by punishing weak-side help vertically rather than horizontally.

tags.name: Savvy Cutter
tags.description: Reads holes in the defense and cuts on time. Creates value without the ball or a shot.

tags.name: Manipulative Passer
tags.description: Moves defenders with eyes, shoulders, pump fakes. Unlocks reads other passers can't see.

tags.name: Wizardly Passer
tags.description: High-degree-of-difficulty deliveries — wraparounds, over-the-shoulder, no-looks. The dazzling layer.

tags.name: Live-Dribble Passer
tags.description: Creates assists off the bounce — kickouts to the corner, drop-offs through traffic. Pick-and-roll engine.

tags.name: Ball Pressure
tags.description: On-ball harassment from 94 feet. Pressures the dribbler, forces difficult initiations, flips possessions.

tags.name: Assignment Defender
tags.description: Takes the toughest matchup and holds. POA defense with positional discipline; the role-player two-way label.

tags.name: Off-Ball Disruptor
tags.description: Generates events from the help side — deflections, recoveries, weak-side blocks, steal-to-leak.

tags.name: Defensive Anchor
tags.description: The five who organizes the scheme. Verticality at the rim, communication, rotation timing.

tags.name: Switchable
tags.description: Holds up against multiple positions. Can guard 1-4 (or 2-5) without becoming a target in switches.

tags.name: Bendy
tags.description: Flexibility, hip mobility, change-of-direction. The athletic profile that translates well across schemes.

tags.name: Immovable Object
tags.description: Strong base, anchor in the paint, holds position against bigger bodies. Won't get bullied.

tags.name: Motor
tags.description: Plays harder than the rest. Energy + effort show up on every possession; rare drops in intensity.

tags.name: Rebounding
tags.description: Wins boards out of position. Tracks misses, boxes out, finishes plays. Translatable in any scheme.

tags.name: Little Thing King
tags.description: Charges, screen assists, deflections, hockey assists. The plus column for everything that doesn't show up in box scores.

tags.name: Pantheon Potential
tags.description: Generational ceiling. 3-5 per class, top of board only.
tags.usageGuide: Reserve for prospects with a real shot at the all-time conversation. Should feel weighty when assigned.

tags.name: Late Bloomer
tags.description: 22+ year old with a sharp recent climb. The under-the-radar bet that just put pieces together.
tags.usageGuide: Use sparingly — must be 22+ AND have a visible jump (year-over-year, transfer, or post-injury). 5-10 per class.

tags.name: Lives for the Moment
tags.description: Confirmed big-game performer. Tournament, late-clock, hostile road — the moment makes them better.
tags.usageGuide: Needs evidence — big-game tape, late-game results, real stakes. ~10 per class.

tags.name: Swing Pick
tags.description: Single-variable bet. One thing has to translate for the pick to hit — and that one thing is plausible but uncertain.
tags.usageGuide: Use when there's a clear unlock condition ("if the shot translates", "if the frame fills out"). ~10 per class.

tags.name: Plug-and-Play
tags.description: Ready now. The role is clear, the translation isn't in question, they can be on the floor in October.
tags.usageGuide: Skews older + role-clear. ~15 per class. Mutually exclusive with Swing Pick.

tags.name: Two-Way Bet
tags.description: Genuine both-ends impact projected. Not just "tries on defense" — the read is they'll move the needle on both ends.
tags.usageGuide: Both-ends signal must be real, not aspirational. ~10 per class.

tags.name: Injury Concerns
tags.description: History of significant injuries, current chronic issue, or red-flag medicals. Long-term availability is a real question.

tags.name: Frame Concern
tags.description: Body has to fill out for the projection to land. Wingspan / weight / lower-body strength is a known limiter.

tags.name: Shot Question
tags.description: Shooting is the unknown variable. Whether it's mechanics, sample size, or live-action vs catch-and-shoot, the read isn't settled.

tags.name: Age Concern
tags.description: Older than peers without a corresponding output edge. Less runway for development; expected to be closer-to-finished now.
